sentences.text: Good morning!
Can you hear me?
I can hear you now!
How are you?
I'm good, how are you?
Good!
I am doing alright.
I took… I took some time off this week, and, you know, we have a 4-day week next week, so I'm… I'm doing okay.
Yeah.
Remind me where you're located.
Are you in Louisiana?
Yes, in New Orleans.

In New Orleans, I love New Orleans so much, and my, … my mom's mom was born on a houseboat in a bayou of Lake Charles, and her first language was Cajun French, so… I have a little… I have a little… a little heritage in the area, but I… it's… I need to make it back to New Orleans.
It's been so long.
Are you… are you born and raised New Orleans?
Yes.
Okay, lovely!
Well, that's amazing.
Well, I appreciate your time.
Yeah.
And I do have Grayson here with me.
She's our Digital Media Manager, so….
Okay!
does everything, digital for us, like Google and all of our social media ads, and also just, like, our brand reputation.
Okay, okay, perfect.
Well, I'm really excited, to show y'all everything.
We can definitely focus more on, like, the Google and the reputation side of things today.
But I guess I'm kind of curious, because I did see that y'all were doing some posting.
Is that… Grace, are you doing all the posts?
Kind of a mix.
So, like, it depends on what you're looking at.
So, if you're looking at… we have… we do have another product with Zumper for posting on a couple… I think two of our properties, but mainly, yes, it is Grace or myself doing the posting.
Okay, okay, got it.
Alright, so Zumper is posting on two properties.
And remind me, you're with Rentable, right?
This is….
So, yes, it was Rentable, and then we rebranded, earlier this year to Maven AI.
So, are y'all familiar with Rentable's Google product back in the day?
Yes and no.
I mean, we've met with multiple rentable reps throughout the years, you know, and heard the spiel.
We have used them before, but … the Google part, that's why, like, that interested me.
That's why I booked this appointment.

Yeah, so… and honestly, a huge reason why we did rebrand is because, you know, Rentable had obviously been around longer than our Google Business Profile automation tool, so a lot of the times when people heard Rentable, they would think, you know, ILS, advertising, and yeah, now we've, you know, expanded so much on, like, our SEO product suite.
We've additionally… I mean, we've made so many updates to our Google Business tool, so it will likely look different.
I did try to take a peek, it looks like maybe you spoke with Austin Hansen last February.
So, yeah, there are definitely updates that I'll show you.
But okay, great!
Yes, so… The two properties that you have with Zumper, they're just doing posting, is that correct?
Yeah, they're doing posting, and then they're also doing, like, we have them synced with our Appfolio, so we have the real-time pricing showing.
Okay, okay, got it.
Then I must be looking at a profile that you guys really are just truly posting every day.
What property are you looking at?
Very impressive.
I am looking at Alluvia, is that how you say it?
That is through Zumper, yes.
That is through Zumper.
Okay, got it, because, yeah, I didn't see the pricing… oh, there it is.
Okay, got it, got it, got it.
Okay, perfect.
Alright, thank you so much.
Okay, so Zumper's doing the two, and then y'all are posting, as much as you can on the remainder.
How many profile… or properties are in the portfolio overall?
75, okay, and are they all, assets, or do y'all do third party?
No, they're all assets.
We actually build and maintain and manage all of our communities.
Okay, amazing.
So, with that being said, across 75 properties, y'all's hands are probably very full on the marketing side.
Yeah, absolutely.
It's that uncle moment.
Okay, got it.

And then with the Google Business Profiles, is there anything, in particular that… that Zumper is not doing that you kind of wish that they would, or is there any reason why you, you know, were interested in kind of taking the call, to see how we compare to Zumper?
So I'll be obvious, totally honest with you, anything Google right now is catching my eye.
You know, obviously Google has always been a main focus of ours, and it's always something that, you know, is on our radar.

But with all the changes in ChatGPT and all of the, … you know, just the algorithm changes in general, and everything going on with Google, and the way that they're starting to favor different profiles over others based on certain criteria.

Anytime I see Google, it really catches my eyes and draws me in, so I'm always interested in learning what's new out there, what are we not doing, what should we be taking advantage of, and just, you know, hearing what other companies are saying about Google and, like, what they have to offer.
One of the things that, you know, we're really struggling with as far as our Google business profiles is.

whenever we try and make certain changes on our Google Business profiles, we are getting flagged, and we're re-triggering verification, which is something that is a huge struggle for us, because obviously, like, you know, they did have some changes with categories, and we want to put our category, make sure that we're the right thing, but every time we try and change one category on a property, that property is automatically re-triggered for verification.
Oh my god.

Yeah, so it's been… it's been a struggle, and our re-verification process, you know, we've tried doing the videos, we've done all, like, the help things, and watched all the videos to make sure that we're doing it correctly, and for some reason, Google still doesn't like it, so now we're kind of in this loop of, trying to get some of our properties back online.
Basically.
Oh, sorry, go ahead.
Go ahead, keep going.
I was just gonna say, it is so wild, like, how Google Alphabet is such a huge company, and I'm always like, where is y'all support?
Like, where are you guys?
Yeah, okay, so how many… how many profiles are you experiencing that with currently?
Everybody has, like, 4?
Yeah.
Okay, okay, that's not too bad.
I… I mean, yes, still an absolute pain, but I was so worried it was going to be a larger number.
Well, we don't let it be uncertain.
Yeah, like, we're tiptoeing, like, we're not, you know, making… That we wanted to make, but we… we do want to make them, we just can't.
Unrelated to this entire conversation, there is someone on our team who is very familiar with, working with Google Business Profiles that I'd be happy to ask her if she's run up against this before.
May I ask what the category is, just so maybe I can, like, see if I can find anything out to just email y'all back at the end of the call?
Yeah, I'd have to find it really quick.
I mean, I just want to make sure I'm saying the right thing.

Yeah, and if you want to email me back later, we absolutely can do that, but I am curious to see if there is something, that we can do, because I know, like, in specific, and I know that you mentioned it's a new… a new category that Google's flagging, but I know that, like.
Well, I don't know that they're flagging it per category, it's more that, you know, like, we're trying to change the category to apartment complex, so some of them have….
Okay.
I'm looking at, like, rental agency, and it kept reverting back, so we would change it back to apartment.
And then it would, like, it got flagged on some of them.
I wanted you to put rental agency instead of apartment complex?
Yeah.
I don't know how it changed, like, it….
Google suggests, it likes to suggest what you should do.
Yeah.
But it's not a suggestion, because they just change it.
Right, right, it's like, well, you didn't give me much time to think it over, did you?
Okay, alright, let me see if there's anything that I can figure out on that front.
But okay, I'm sorry that you guys are dealing with that.
So, yes, I understand that's kind of been an issue, and of course, you know.
What we're gonna be doing is similar to Zumper, so yeah, we would integrate with AppFolio to do the same… same things to some degree on the profile.
We have a little bit more optimization than Zumper, and then I'm confident we could beat their price.

But yeah, as far as, like, the listings information, you know, ultimately that will be in y'all's wheelhouse, but yeah, let me see if there's anything that I can figure out for y'all, just, you know, one-off, because that is very frustrating.
I'm so sorry.
Okay.
So, with Zumper, … Do y'all have anything else through them, or are they just doing the Google for the two profiles?
I mean, not… not necessarily, no.
Okay, so not doing, like, reputation or listings or anything?
No, we manage our reputation through BirdEye.
BirdEye!
Okay, got it.
We white-label BirdEye.
So very familiar with that platform, that's a great platform.
Alright, and then you said your property management software is Appfolio?
Yes.
Okay, perfect, alright.
And just so you know, while we do white-label BirdEye, this Google Business Profile Automation tool is our proprietary technology, so did just want to make… make a little note of that there.
But do y'all have any questions before I hop in?
I don't think so, no.
Okay, okay, great.
So, yes, … as I know that y'all just touched on, Google is super important in the year 2025, even more so.

I don't know if y'all have been to NAA or any of those big conferences, but it's definitely, like, Google business profiles are huge on people's radar right now, and Google, according to Google earlier this year, has Given a lot more weight on Google business profiles in terms of optimizing organic SEO.
So I think that's also been a huge reason why it's been popular, but, as you guys know.
handling a Google business profile on your own is pretty impossible, and then, you know, even with 5, it's nearly impossible to get to.
But, it's still important because about 87% of renters are still starting their search on Google for their new apartment.
So when they come across your Google business profiles.

We, of course, want to make sure that y'all look as great as possible, that, the prospective resident's really able to get an idea of what it's like to live at the property, the amenities, that sort of thing.
But then beyond digital curb appeal, there comes that little SEO piece as well.
So, Google rewards relevant, frequent, and unique content to the profiles.

with an increase in local rank on maps, and KPIs over time, like property website traffic and impressions, we work with, you know, the Camdons, the asset livings of the world, and … what we see in terms of KPIs increasing across the nation are pretty phenomenal with this tool.
So, the reason why we wanted to create this, product suite is because we hear more and more about marketing teams being tasked for stronger results with fewer resources most of the time.

Sometimes on-site teams are tasked to do some of the Google posting, but then, you know, ultimately they can't really get to it as well, and then ultimately that renter experience is not standardized.
So… With that being said, what we do is we integrate with Appfolio to do 4 things on your profiles daily.
So, first and foremost, we will use your photos and leverage AI to create property and amenity-rich content.
We pay attention to SEO keywords, if there are any, like, PPC campaigns you're running, or Google Ads.
We can also incorporate those keywords to give them a little lift.
We pull over the live pricing and availability of, your floor plans right on Google as well.
We also know that fee transparency is kind of where this industry is moving.
It kind of seems to be the case.
So that's something that we can also incorporate as, you know, fee transparency at a super, like, very, low level, if you will, like, application fees, that sort of thing.
Then we help with Q&A management, and we also post about concessions and specials when they are offered.
Okay, so this is one of, Zumper's profiles, then?
Yes.
Okay, okay, perfect!
Alright, so they have… All of that, okay, amazing.
And then the Q&A feed, will you remind me, is that something that Zumper is offering?
They might offer it, but we handle it.
Okay, okay, got it.
Alright, well then, yes, wanted to pull up one of Hamilton… properties using us.
So if we scroll down, first and foremost, we will have that products and availability feed.
So this, of course, similar to Zumper, will integrate with Appfolio and then pull over that live pricing and availability.
If you click in, it's gonna give a little bit more in-depth look of what that floor plan entails.
And then, if you click into the site, it will take us to this property website landing page.
It looks like this property has it per bed, but we certainly can do, you know, of course, the entire floor plan rather than just the bed pricing.
So there's that, and then if we scroll down… Here is going to be those, posts that I mentioned.

So, again, we just want to make sure that we are using as as many amenity-rich keywords as possible, so that when a renter is looking for apartments near me with a fitness center, you're more likely to come up.
But we will be using your photos for this, so, any photos that are in AppFolio, we'll basically just pull up Over, and then create the content accordingly.
You got it.
Any questions on that?
Not yet.
Alright, I do want to make a quick note on here.
Two things on this.
So, under the products feed, what we can additionally do is showcase Any amenities, such as, like, an EV charger, something like that.
Even if you don't have photos on that, we do have some stock photos that we can use.
But that's another way that we can further fill out that products and availability feed.
But then also, anytime you are running concessions or specials, anytime that field is updated in AppFolio, our tool will acknowledge that automatically.
And then, create content around it accordingly.

So we will create a post about it once a week while that special's running, and then once that special expires, we will actually delete the post automatically, just to make sure that no one's referencing an outdated special.

Alright, so lastly, and I know that you just said that y'all are doing… the Q&A in-house, but I did want to show you, again, everything can be turned off or on in the event that this wasn't something that you wanted to have on, but something that we did notice was a lot of the times, questions would be asked, and then, they wouldn't be answered in an appropriate, timely manner, if you will.

So, in order to get ahead of a prospective resident, maybe even needing to ask a question, we looked at the most frequently asked questions to Google business profiles, and we found that the questions that were being asked in the multifamily industry were, of course, about availability, amenities, pet policy, so on and so forth.
So, what we do is we place these questions on your profile.
And then we edit the answer daily.

Even if the answer doesn't change, what we want to do is edit that answer, just so, again, we are signaling to Google, you're a super active business, we're feeding it that relevant, frequent, unique content that it wants to see to the profile.
I'm sorry, can you say that again?
So you add the question, and then you answer it daily?
Yep.
So, for instance, like, this says, when is your office open?
So, that's a question that we have on the profile, but then every day, we're going to, like, as you see right here, it's edited 16 hours ago.
We still edit the answer, because we know that that's how you feed Google's algorithm, is the more information you can feed it.
the more that it will reward you with that, local increase in organic SEO.
That's interesting.
So each day, you're posting essentially the same answer.
So, yes, we'll edit the same answer.
but….
But for, for example, let's see if there's one… okay, so this has all their floor plans available.
But for instance, like, for the availability piece, let's say this says as of August 25th, we don't have 4 bedrooms available today, we expect them to be available at XYZ date.
So….
Where are you getting that information from?
Would that pull from AppFolio?
So, like, especially about availability, I mean, our availability changes, you know?
Yep, so that'll… that'll be straight from AppFolio.
Everything that we pull is going to be from AppFolio.
Okay.
I will say, on that note, did that answer your question, as far as the Q&A goes?
Yeah, it's very interesting.
Okay, perfect.

So yeah, with that being said, everything that we do, we pull from Appfolio, but we understand that, you know, brand guidelines are important for some property management companies, so there are a few ways that we can align to make sure that your brand is properly being reflected over to Google.
So, first and foremost, in the event that this were something They all were to roll out.
Our tools come with a dedicated customer success manager.

So, we would do a kickoff call and make sure that we are aligning on your brand voice, any brand guidelines that you have in place, which could be something as simple as, you know, I met with someone the other day who didn't want anyone to… or didn't want the profile to say, like, apartment.
It would prefer… they preferred to say home.
So something as simple as that.

But then from there, we can also connect with your property websites, and our, our model will basically learn your language, and our posts will get, a little bit more defined over time with your brand voice.
Exactly.
Makes sense.
Yep.
Okay.
Okay, perfect.
And then, out of curiosity, … As far as the reputation management piece goes with BirdEye, is that contract, in place.
Yeah, I mean, we are very happy with BirdEye.
Everyone has tried to give us this feel.
We have not found something that we like better.
You can absolutely tell us what you have, but we are very happy with BirdEye.
Yeah, no, the only reason why I asked, I mean, because truly we do white-label BirdEye, it's even, yeah, it's on… it's on the slide.
The reason why I asked is, sometimes we can actually beat BirdEye's pricing, so I was just curious about that, but no, I love to hear that you're happy with BirdEye.
… Yeah, we have a lot of our partners that are super happy with that tool.
Sorry, I lost my pricing deck.
I'm going to pull that up really quickly, but, I'm curious, kind of, what are your thoughts on all of that?
Yeah, I mean, I'm very interested in the question and answers piece, … I think the rest of it, you know, is stuff that we have seen before.
But the question and answer piece is definitely something new and different that we haven't seen.
Okay.
I'm gonna have to do a little bit more research on my own and see, like, how that would benefit.
Our listings, … But yeah, I mean, definitely, definitely interested in that.
Okay, okay, great.
And, like, as I mentioned, we absolutely can beat Zumper's price.
So the way that we work is, it is priced by property, so we start at $199 a month of property, however, we do offer volume discounts.
So I'm curious, I know that you, of course, mentioned, you know, you want to go and look back at everything and Just make sure that it does, you know, make sense for y'all.
… Is this something that y'all are kind of looking to budget for your whole portfolio going into 2026?
No, no, definitely not our whole portfolio.
I mean, not every single one of our properties has availability, so we're not ever trying to do everything for every single one of our properties.
Looking at the prices you guys have to offer, actually, Zumper beats you guys right now.

So, one of the things that we also love about Zumper is that we Get a listing with them, too, and we see it on our… throughout Folio, we see that that listing is live, so that's an added bonus for us to have Zumper.
… Okay.
But again, no, never, never try to have all 75 on one, one platform.
Okay, okay, yeah, completely understand.

And yeah, no, this is just our list price, but yeah, what I'm… I'm saying that we absolutely can beat Zumper's price, so I'm curious, what are they… what is the cost for the two properties that you have in place with them?
At the moment, I'm not really comfortable telling you, but, I do have some other questions about your pricing and contract.
So, like, what's the shortest-term contract that you guys offer?

Yep, so we do operate on annual agreements, so when it comes to that organic SEO, Feeding Google organically is a little bit more of a long play, so we see those KPIs really start to increase at, like, the 7-9 month mark, so we, of course, want to make sure that our partners are able to see that ROI on the SEO piece, so we do operate on annual agreements.
So, you're saying that, like, we really wouldn't see, an increase in traffic until 7 to 9 months with you guys?

You would see an increase in traffic, but where you're really able to be like, holy moly, okay, this is up, you know, 40%, that's the sweet spot that we really start to see that Google rewards that SEO piece.
And so, how do you guys measure success?
How do you measure or track if the product is working for a property?
Yeah, absolutely, so that's kind of up to y'all.
So I would say that the majority of our partners do measure success in terms of, of course, making sure that those profiles are optimized with those four features that we do on the profile daily.
But then, yes, we do have partners who will measure success as well with, like.
for instance, a KPI that we track is going to be, that website traffic, so we do have a lot of partners that will kind of… measure the success with, the increase in traffic over time.
But yeah, the way that it works is everything that we have on our end, you would be able to see on your end as well.

We, of course, could pull those reports, when you wanted, and I can send you… what I'll do after this is I will send over, a KPI report for someone who has properties in the LA, Mississippi area, and send that over so you can see.
But yes, I would say that's a few different ways that our partners do measure success.
Okay.
And then, since it is an annual agreement, what's like a buyout?
Let's say we decided that we don't want our property on Maven AI anymore for whatever reason after we're, you know, 6 months in.
What is… how do we end that contract?
I mean, I can definitely see if… we can get something in place.
I guess I have a few questions around that, like, what would… What would… can you give me an example of why you would terminate the product at a property?
I mean, in a perfect world, like, whatever property we had this for, they became totally, totally full.
Like, there was no availability, no occupancy.
I mean, no availability, full occupancy, so… You know, we wouldn't need it anymore.
So, this tool, it's not really a lead-generating tool.

Like, Google is more so, again, kind of like… That organic SEO piece, so, yeah, as far as occupancy goes, like, we have a lot of partners who, even though they are at… you know, maximum occupancy, they still have this tool in place, to continue to make sure that they are increased on, you know, local maps when someone searches apartments near me.
So… so, yes.
Unfortunately, that isn't a way that we would put Look at the tools not working properly.
And then, is there any sort of platform to manage the posts, or manage the questions?

I know that one of the… one of the concerns that we often have is, we see a post that Zumper did, or on our Google listings that like you mentioned, don't match our brand voice, or just… is a picture that we don't want posted, so is there a place that we can manage this?
Absolutely.
Let me pull that up one second.
This is a newer, dashboard to us, so let me pull this up really quickly.
We are… Right now, giving mostly, existing customers this access, but I definitely think that I would be able to get y'all access on this, so let me pull this up really quick, I'm so sorry.

So, the way that this works… is, you would be given a login, of course, and then let me share my screen so you can see this, and apologies, I am not sure which… property management company I have pulled up.
But this is our newest dashboard, which we've been getting really, really great feedback on.
We will go ahead… we can, help with Facebook and Instagram as well, in the event that is something that y'all were looking at down the road, but for the purpose of this demo.

I will have just the Google Business post up, so… you would be given this dashboard access that would allow you to see both the scheduled posts, so these are the upcoming posts that are going to be scheduled.

So to answer your question, if you did look at this, and for whatever reason you're like, you know, I don't really love this, you can either create your own text, you know, you can edit it where you see fit.
Or, you can additionally just completely regenerate the post altogether for us for our tool to… to regenerate.
So those are two different ways that you could kind of handle that.
Does that answer your question?
Do you have any questions on that?
Yeah, no, that's great.
Okay, okay, perfect.
And then you can also see, additionally, the published posts.

You can either, like, bulk select properties if you wanted, if you wanted to just look at one property, what, you know, had been published on Google and what's upcoming, you can absolutely do that as well.
But then, you know, with Google.
anytime you did want to create your own content, you absolutely could.

I would say, you know, with content creation that's more community-specific, I would say that's really great for Facebook and Instagram, because of course, that's where your residents kind of live, more so than, you know, looking at your Google Business profile, but if you did want to kind of highlight how y'all were a community-oriented property.
Let's say y'all have, like, a movie night coming up or something like that.

You could select the property that you wanted to create a post on, we'll select Google, you can, you know, drag and drop a photo where you see fit, create your own content, and then you can either create the post immediately, or you could schedule out the post as well.
So, a few different ways to manage Google on y'all's end.
But then, of course, ideally, the tool really is set it and forget it.
So yeah, ideally, we would align on the front end, you know, try to make sure that we do define your brand language as best as possible, so y'all didn't have to be in here as much.
But, yes, that is what your visibility looks like as far as the upcoming posts and, how y'all are able to edit them.
Okay, great.
Yeah!
Okay.

So then, yes, I don't think I would have any issue giving y'all access to that dashboard, so I will be sure to make a note of that, just on my end, in the event that this were something for y'all to roll out.
… But yeah, I guess, what are your thoughts after seeing the dash?
Is there, a good time to maybe follow up?
Yeah, the dashboard's great, … I…
I think… I mean, we are happy with Zumper right now, so I don't know that we're ready to switch, but it's good to always see what else is out there, …
I definitely don't think that we would be using or switching in the next month or two, so… But it's good to know that it's out there, and if we do change our mind, I'll definitely reach out.
Okay, yeah, absolutely, because, as I mentioned, we can beat Zumper's price by 5%.

I know that that's something that my director would be comfortable with me offering, so we can absolutely beat whatever Zumper is offering you by 5%, and then, we can also delay billing for a few months, just to make sure that there isn't any overlap with the contract as well.
So… Yes, I'd be happy to offer that price.
I know, I know that, I'm not sure what the price is currently, so I can just make a note of that in the follow-up.
But then, yes, would there be a good time to follow up with you and Grace?
I mean, you can definitely reach out to us.
I… within the next month, but, I don't foresee us changing within… at least… at least definitely not in the next month, we'll be changing.
Okay!

Okay, alright, well then, yeah, I will definitely, just send over little pieces of, you know, information, KPI report, so you're able to see how, other property management companies in the area have performed on the tool, and then, yeah, let me know if you or anyone else has any questions or wants to see a demo.
Absolutely, thank you so much.
Yeah, thank you, have a good one.
Get them.
